Tests the behavior of JavaScript's Math.pow function with negative infinity and negative exponent.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests a mathematical operation in JavaScript. It checks if dividing 1 by the result of raising negative infinity to the power of -6 equals positive infinity. The use of `Math.pow` and `Infinity` indicates that the code is testing the behavior of JavaScript's mathematical functions and constants when handling special numeric values. This is a narrow functionality test, likely intended to verify the correctness of JavaScript's handling of edge cases in mathematical computations.
